node.text:
The Infertility Inheritance Paradox 🏛️🧬🤯

​A "profound" discovery that collapses under the weight of basic biology. This viral 2026 exchange from "Technically the Truth" highlights one of the most hilarious logical fallacies currently trending on social media.

​The "insight" from user KYTON claims that infertility is genetic, asserting that "if your parents couldn't have kids, you also won't." The immediate paradox, as pointed out by the sarcastic "dealer's number" rebuttal, is that if the premise were true, the subject wouldn't exist to inherit the trait in the first place. In 2026 internet culture, this is known as a Circular Logic Trap: a statement where the conclusion (your existence) is canceled out by its own requirement (parents who can't reproduce). It’s a 2026 reminder that even the most "scientific" sounding claims can be completely derailed by a single second of logical scrutiny. 

​Is this the most "confidentially incorrect" statement of the year, or is there a hidden layer of genius we’re missing? 🧬👇
.
.
#logic #biology #memes #paradox #science
Sure. If my parents didn’t have kids, I couldn’t possibly have kids since I wouldn’t exist.
